# Tree Schema

::: {.callout-note}
## Complete Reference
This page provides an overview of MolViewSpec node types. For the complete, authoritative schema specification, see the [Official MolViewSpec Tree Schema](https://molstar.org/mol-view-spec/docs/tree-schema/).
:::

## What is the Tree Schema?

The MolViewSpec tree schema defines all available node types, their parameters, and parent-child relationships. When you use the builder API in MolViewStories, you're constructing a tree of these nodes.

## Node Hierarchy Overview

This diagram shows the complete MolViewSpec node tree structure and all possible parent-child relationships:

```sh
root
├── camera (global camera position)
├── canvas (background color)
├── download (load data from URL)
│   └── parse (parse file format)
│       ├── structure (create 3D structure)
│       │   ├── component (select part of structure)
│       │   │   ├── representation (visual style)
│       │   │   │   ├── color (inline color)
│       │   │   │   ├── color_from_uri (color from external file)
│       │   │   │   ├── color_from_source (color from mmCIF)
│       │   │   │   ├── label (inline label)
│       │   │   │   ├── label_from_uri (labels from external file)
│       │   │   │   ├── label_from_source (labels from mmCIF)
│       │   │   │   ├── tooltip (inline tooltip)
│       │   │   │   ├── tooltip_from_uri (tooltips from external file)
│       │   │   │   ├── tooltip_from_source (tooltips from mmCIF)
│       │   │   │   └── opacity (transparency control)
│       │   │   ├── focus (auto-frame component)
│       │   │   └── transform (rotate/translate)
│       │   ├── component_from_uri (components from external file)
│       │   │   └── representation (same children as component)
│       │   └── component_from_source (components from mmCIF)
│       │       └── representation (same children as component)
│       └── volume (volumetric data)
│           └── volume_representation (isosurface)
│               ├── color
│               └── opacity
└── primitives (custom geometry group)
    ├── primitive (individual shape)
    │   ├── arrow
    │   ├── box
    │   ├── ellipse
    │   ├── ellipsoid
    │   ├── sphere
    │   ├── tube
    │   ├── distance_measurement
    │   ├── angle_measurement
    │   ├── label
    │   ├── lines (custom line geometry)
    │   └── mesh (custom triangle mesh)
    └── primitives_from_uri (load primitives from file)
```

### Representation Types

When using `representation()`, these types are available:
- `ball_and_stick` - Atomic detail
- `cartoon` - Secondary structure
- `surface` - Molecular surface
- `spacefill` - Van der Waals spheres
- `carbohydrate` - Carbohydrate symbols
- `isosurface` - Volumetric isosurface (for volumes only)

### Primitive Types

When using `primitive()`, these kinds are available:
- `arrow` - Directional arrow
- `box` - Box shape
- `ellipse` - 2D ellipse
- `ellipsoid` - 3D ellipsoid/sphere
- `sphere` - Sphere (shorthand for ellipsoid)
- `tube` - Cylinder/tube
- `distance_measurement` - Distance with label
- `angle_measurement` - Angle with arc and label
- `label` - Floating 3D text label
- `lines` - Custom line geometry
- `mesh` - Custom triangle mesh

## Core Node Types

### Data Loading Nodes

#### `download`
Loads data from a URL.

**Parameters**:
- `url` (required): Data resource URL
- `id` (optional): Identifier for the download

**Example**:
```javascript
builder.download({ url: 'https://files.rcsb.org/download/1ABC.cif' })
```

#### `parse`
Parses downloaded data.

**Parameters**:
- `format` (required): File format
  - Structure formats: `"mmcif"`, `"bcif"`, `"pdb"`, `"pdbqt"`, `"gro"`, `"xyz"`, `"mol"`, `"sdf"`, `"mol2"`
  - Trajectory formats: `"lammpstrj"`, `"xtc"`, `"nctraj"`, `"dcd"`, `"trr"`
  - Topology formats: `"psf"`, `"prmtop"`, `"top"`
  - Volume formats: `"map"`, `"dx"`, `"dxbin"`

**Example**:
```javascript
.parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
```

### Structure Nodes

#### `structure`
Creates a molecular structure representation.

**Parameters**:
- `type`: Structure type
  - `"model"` - Original coordinates
  - `"assembly"` - Biological assembly
  - `"symmetry"` - Crystal symmetry
  - `"symmetry_mates"` - Nearby asymmetric units
- `model_index`: Which model to use (0-based)
- `assembly_id`: Assembly identifier (for assemblies)
- `block_header`/`block_index`: CIF block selection
- `radius`: Radius for symmetry mates (Angstroms)
- `ijk_min`/`ijk_max`: Miller indices for crystal symmetry

**Example**:
```javascript
.modelStructure()  // Shorthand for type: "model"
.assemblyStructure({ assembly_id: '1' })
.symmetryStructure({ ijk_min: [-1, -1, -1], ijk_max: [1, 1, 1] })
```

#### `component`
Selects a subset of the structure.

**Parameters**:
- `selector`: [Selection expression](selectors.qmd)
  - Static: `"protein"`, `"ligand"`, `"water"`, etc.
  - Component expression: `{ label_asym_id: 'A', label_seq_id: 100 }`
  - Union: Array of component expressions

**Example**:
```javascript
.component({ selector: 'protein' })
.component({ selector: { label_asym_id: 'A' } })
.component({ selector: [{ label_seq_id: 10 }, { label_seq_id: 20 }] })
```

#### `component_from_uri` / `component_from_source`
Creates components from [annotation files](annotations.qmd).

**Parameters**:
- `uri`: File URL (for `_from_uri`)
- `format`: `"json"`, `"cif"`, `"bcif"`
- `schema`: Annotation schema
- `block_header`/`category_name`/`field_name`: For CIF/BCIF

### Representation Nodes

#### `representation`
Defines how to visualize a component.

**Parameters**:
- `type` (required): Visual style
  - `"cartoon"` - Secondary structure
  - `"ball_and_stick"` - Atomic detail
  - `"surface"` - Molecular surface
  - `"spacefill"` - Van der Waals spheres
  - `"backbone"` - CA trace
  - `"gaussian_surface"` - Smooth surface
  - `"point"` - Point cloud
  - `"putty"` - Thickness-varied cartoon
  - `"label"` - Text labels

**Example**:
```javascript
.representation({ type: 'cartoon' })
.representation({ type: 'ball_and_stick' })
.representation({ type: 'surface' })
```

### Coloring Nodes

#### `color`
Applies color to representation.

**Parameters**:
- `color`: Color value
  - Named: `"red"`, `"blue"`, `"green"`
  - Hex: `"#ff0000"`
  - RGB: `"rgb(255, 0, 0)"`
  - Scheme: `"element-symbol"`, `"chain-id"`, `"secondary-structure"`, `"b-factor"`
- `selector` (optional): Apply to specific atoms

**Example**:
```javascript
.color({ color: 'red' })
.color({ color: '#3498db' })
.color({ color: 'chain-id' })
.color({ selector: { label_asym_id: 'A' }, color: 'blue' })
```

#### `color_from_uri` / `color_from_source`
Applies colors from [annotation files](annotations.qmd).

**Parameters**:
- `uri`: File URL (for `_from_uri`)
- `format`: `"json"`, `"cif"`, `"bcif"`
- `schema`: Annotation schema
- `block_header`/`category_name`/`field_name`: For CIF/BCIF

### Label and Tooltip Nodes

#### `label`
Adds text label to component.

**Parameters**:
- `text`: Label text

**Example**:
```javascript
.label({ text: 'Active Site' })
```

#### `label_from_uri` / `label_from_source`
Adds labels from [annotation files](annotations.qmd).

#### `tooltip`
Adds hover tooltip to component.

**Parameters**:
- `text`: Tooltip text

**Example**:
```javascript
.tooltip({ text: 'Catalytic residue' })
```

#### `tooltip_from_uri` / `tooltip_from_source`
Adds tooltips from [annotation files](annotations.qmd).

### Camera Node

#### `camera`
Controls camera position and orientation.

**Parameters**:
- `target`: Look-at point `[x, y, z]`
- `position`: Camera position `[x, y, z]`
- `up`: Up direction `[x, y, z]`

**Example**:
```javascript
builder.camera({
    target: [0, 0, 0],
    position: [50, 50, 50],
    up: [0, 1, 0]
})
```

See [Camera Settings](camera.qmd) for details.

### Canvas Node

#### `canvas`
Sets canvas background color.

**Parameters**:
- `background_color`: Color value

**Example**:
```javascript
builder.canvas({ background_color: '#f0f0f0' })
```

### Transform Nodes

#### `transform`
Applies geometric transformation.

**Parameters**:
- `rotation`: 3×3 rotation matrix
- `translation`: Translation vector `[x, y, z]`

**Example**:
```javascript
.transform({
    rotation: [[1, 0, 0], [0, 1, 0], [0, 0, 1]],
    translation: [10, 0, 0]
})
```

## Parameter Types

- **String**: Text value, e.g., `"mmcif"`, `"cartoon"`
- **Number**: Numeric value, e.g., `42`, `3.14`
- **Integer**: Whole number, e.g., `0`, `1`, `100`
- **Boolean**: `true` or `false`
- **Array**: List of values, e.g., `[1, 2, 3]`
- **Object**: Key-value pairs, e.g., `{ label_asym_id: 'A' }`
- **Optional**: Marked with `?`, can be omitted

## Common Patterns

### Basic Protein Visualization

```javascript
const structure = builder
    .download({ url: 'https://files.rcsb.org/download/1ABC.cif' })
    .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
    .modelStructure();

structure
    .component({ selector: 'protein' })
    .representation({ type: 'cartoon' })
    .color({ color: 'secondary-structure' });
```

### Protein with Ligand

```javascript
// Protein as cartoon
structure
    .component({ selector: 'protein' })
    .representation({ type: 'cartoon' })
    .color({ color: 'chain-id' });

// Ligand as ball-and-stick
structure
    .component({ selector: 'ligand' })
    .representation({ type: 'ball_and_stick' })
    .color({ color: 'element-symbol' });
```

### Chain-Specific Coloring

```javascript
structure
    .component({ selector: 'protein' })
    .representation({ type: 'cartoon' })
    .color({ selector: { label_asym_id: 'A' }, color: 'red' })
    .color({ selector: { label_asym_id: 'B' }, color: 'blue' })
    .color({ selector: { label_asym_id: 'C' }, color: 'green' });
```

### Assembly Structure

```javascript
const assembly = builder
    .download({ url: 'https://files.rcsb.org/download/1ABC.cif' })
    .parse({ format: 'mmcif' })
    .assemblyStructure({ assembly_id: '1' });

assembly
    .component({ selector: 'polymer' })
    .representation({ type: 'cartoon' });
```

## See Also

- [Selectors](selectors.qmd) - Selection syntax reference
- [Annotations](annotations.qmd) - External annotation files
- [Camera Settings](camera.qmd) - Camera control
- [Official Tree Schema](https://molstar.org/mol-view-spec/docs/tree-schema/) - Complete specification
